description_long: This is a single-story wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles and a stone foundation. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal red siding, giving it a classic rustic appearance. The front facade features a wide covered porch with wooden railings and steps leading up to the entrance, providing a welcoming entryway. Windows are symmetrically placed on the visible sides, with two on the main facade and one on each gable end, all rectangular in shape. A small chimney protrudes from the roof, suggesting the presence of a fireplace or stove inside. The cabin's design is simple yet functional, suitable for residential use in suburban or park-like urban settings.
description: A red wooden cabin with a gray gable roof, stone foundation, and a wide covered front porch with wooden railings.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the cabin with two rectangular windows and the main entrance porch partially visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gable end with a single window and the side extension of the porch wrapping around the cabin.
description_view_2: This is the front view, showcasing the full width of the covered porch, central entrance, and two front windows.
description_view_3: This view displays the opposite gable end, which is solid with no windows or doors, and the roof's overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in gray asphalt shingles, featuring a single chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: Four rectangular windows: two symmetrically placed on the front facade, one on each gable end; all windows are of similar size and evenly distributed
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade, accessed via the covered porch
